title: Regent Seven Seas South America Cruises 2026
intro: Regent Seven Seas Cruises elevates South America exploration to unprecedented luxury, combining ultra-all-inclusive indulgence with immersive cultural discovery. From the thundering Iguazu Falls to the windswept beauty of Patagonia, guests traverse this magnificent continent aboard intimate, all-suite vessels where every stateroom features a private balcony. The cruise line's complimentary unlimited shore excursions unlock authentic experiences—wine tasting in Mendoza's vineyards, tango lessons in Buenos Aires, and wildlife encounters in the Falkland Islands. With airfare, fine dining at specialty restaurants, premium spirits, gratuities, and one-night pre-cruise hotel stays included, Regent removes every logistical concern. The 2026 season captures South America's dramatic contrasts: Amazon rainforest mysteries, Antarctic gateway voyages, and Chilean fjord navigation through glacier-carved channels. For discerning travelers seeking genuine destination immersion without sacrificing comfort, Regent Seven Seas represents the pinnacle of South American cruising.

faqs.question: How much does a Regent Seven Seas South America cruise cost?
faqs.answer: Regent Seven Seas South America cruises represent the ultra-luxury segment, with fares starting from EUR 5,699 per person for 10-12 night sailings in balcony suites during shoulder season. Peak December-February voyages typically range EUR 6,899-8,999, while expedition-style Antarctica-inclusive itineraries command EUR 12,499-15,999. The top-tier Regent Suite exceeds EUR 50,000 for extended voyages. These prices reflect genuine value: business class airfare, unlimited shore excursions, all beverages, gratuities, and pre-cruise hotel stays are included—components that would cost EUR 3,000-5,000 extra on premium lines. Early booking incentives and past guest loyalty discounts of 5-10% further enhance value.

faqs.question: Which Regent Seven Seas ships sail to South America?
faqs.answer: Three vessels comprise Regent's South America deployment. Seven Seas Explorer, the flagship launched 2016, operates primarily 12-14 night Brazil-Argentina routes with the most spacious suites and elaborate public spaces. Seven Seas Mariner, extensively refurbished 2022, handles longer expedition voyages including Antarctica and Amazon itineraries; its smaller size enables navigation of narrower Chilean fjords. Seven Seas Splendor, the newest 2020 build, alternates between South America and Caribbean positioning voyages. All three feature all-suite, all-balcony configurations with minimum 28 square meter accommodations, ensuring every guest enjoys ocean views regardless of fare paid.

faqs.question: When is best time to cruise South America?
faqs.answer: The optimal South America cruise season spans November through March, coinciding with austral summer when temperatures range 20-30°C and precipitation is minimal. December-February represents peak demand with longest daylight hours and optimal wildlife viewing—penguin colonies peak January-February, while Amazon water levels suit river navigation November-April. Patagonia and Antarctica routes strictly operate December-February when Drake Passage conditions are most favorable. November and March offer 15-20% lower fares with nearly equivalent weather, though some southern ports may experience occasional rain. Avoid April-October when many itineraries suspend operations and Antarctic voyages become impossible due to ice.

faqs.question: What is included?
faqs.answer: Regent's ultra-all-inclusive fare encompasses substantially more than competitors. Roundtrip business class airfare from 29 European gateway cities eliminates international flight costs. Every suite features private balconies, marble bathrooms, and butler service in upper categories. Unlimited shore excursions—typically 3-5 options per port—include exclusive experiences like private estate visits and culinary workshops. All dining spans main restaurant, buffets, and specialty venues including Prime 7 steakhouse and Chartreuse French cuisine without surcharge. Unlimited beverages cover premium spirits, vintage wines, and in-suite minibar replenishment. Pre-cruise luxury hotel nights, ground transfers, WiFi, valet laundry, and gratuities complete the inclusions, leaving virtually no onboard expenses except spa and casino.

faqs.question: Which ports?
faqs.answer: Regent's South America itineraries encompass remarkably diverse port portfolios. Atlantic routes feature Brazilian highlights: Rio de Janeiro's iconic coastline, Salvador's colonial Pelourinho district, and Amazon gateway Santarem. Buenos Aires serves as primary embarkation hub with overnight stays enabling tango immersion. Pacific itineraries navigate Chile's fjord systems calling Puerto Montt, Puerto Chacabuco, and Punta Arenas with glacier cruising between ports. Ushuaia, the world's southernmost city, provides Antarctic expedition embarkation. Uruguay's Montevideo and Punta del Este offer sophisticated beach culture. Select voyages include Falkland Islands' Port Stanley for wildlife photography. The 2026 season introduces expanded Peruvian calls including Lima and Paracas for Nazca Lines overflight extensions.

faqs.question: How far ahead to book?
faqs.answer: Regent's South America inventory demands 12-18 month advance booking for optimal results. The limited 700-750 passenger capacity per vessel means popular suite categories—particularly Master and Grand Suites with expanded balconies—frequently sell out 9-12 months before sailing. Early booking incentives typically offer EUR 500-1,500 savings per suite or complimentary land programs valued at EUR 800-1,200. Past guests with Seven Seas Society status gain 90-day advance reservation windows. For Antarctica-inclusive voyages, 18-24 month booking horizons are prudent given extreme demand. Final payment falls 150 days before departure, substantially earlier than mass-market lines, reflecting the luxury segment's planning requirements.

faqs.question: Good for families?
faqs.answer: Regent Seven Seas presents a nuanced family proposition: exceptional for multigenerational groups and mature teenagers, less suited for young children. The cruise line imposes minimum age six months (12 months for Antarctica) but offers no dedicated children's facilities, kids' clubs, or youth programming. Families appreciate the all-inclusive simplicity—no surprise charges for excursions or beverages—and spacious multi-room suites accommodating up to six guests. Shore excursions engage curious teenagers with authentic cultural immersion unavailable on mainstream lines. However, the sophisticated dining, evening entertainment, and predominantly adult demographic (median age 55-60) may isolate younger children. Regent occasionally offers third-guest reduced fares, making multi-generational travel financially viable despite luxury positioning.

faqs.question: Cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Regent Seven Seas maintains stricter cancellation terms reflecting luxury segment norms and extended advance booking patterns. Cancellations 151+ days before departure incur EUR 250 administrative fee per person. 150-121 days prior forfeits 25% of cruise fare; 120-91 days sacrifices 50%; 90-61 days loses 75%; and 60 days or less results in 100% forfeiture. These thresholds apply substantially earlier than mass-market lines where full penalties often begin 90 days pre-cruise. Travel insurance is strongly recommended—Regent offers proprietary plans covering cancellation for covered reasons, medical evacuation, and trip interruption. 'Cancel For Any Reason' upgrades reimburse 75% of non-refundable costs when standard cancellation motives don't apply, protecting substantial South America investment against unforeseen circumstances.
